evaluation.viewing_angle_source: The source figure shows a 3D stem plot from an elevated orthographic perspective. The vertical axis is positioned at the far-left corner of the grid, while the m and n axes extend forward and to the right, forming a clean coordinate box.
evaluation.viewing_angle_rendered: The rendered screenshot shows the grid rotated such that the vertical axis line is positioned near the center-back of the visual field. This causes the vertical axis and its labels to overlap with the stems and grid lines.
evaluation.viewing_angle_comparison: The rendered viewpoint is rotated too far to the right, which shifts the vertical axis into the center of the plot and causes significant visual overlap and clutter compared to the clean layout of the source.
discrepancies: The vertical axis is placed in the center-back instead of the far-left corner, causing it to overlap with the stems.
The label 'box_{1,1}[n, m]' is placed vertically in the middle of the plot, overlapping a blue stem.
The camera angle causes several stems to line up and overlap, reducing the 3D clarity of the 3x3 grid.
The tick marks and labels for 'm' and 'n' are shifted and do not cleanly line up with the grid edges as in the source.
evaluation.notes: The coordinate system is rotated incorrectly, placing the vertical axis in the center and causing overlapping labels and stems.
action_items: Reposition the camera and adjust the azimuth/elevation to match the source viewpoint exactly (far-left vertical axis).
Move the black vertical axis line and its '0' and '1' ticks to the far-left corner of the grid.
Move the 'box_{1,1}[n, m]' label to the left of the plot so it is completely free from overlapping with the stems.
Add simple interactive toggles (e.g., to change the filter size or show/hide the grid) to improve the interactivity score.

feedback.rationale: The rendered figure has low scores due to an incorrect camera perspective that causes severe axis/stem overlap, misaligned labels, and lack of interactivity, all of which are addressable in refinement.

attempts.html: ⚠ Figure failed to load

evaluation.viewing_angle_source: The source figure shows a 3D stem plot from an elevated orthographic perspective looking down. The vertical value axis is on the far left, the 'm' axis runs downwards and to the left, and the 'n' axis runs downwards and to the right. The 3x3 grid of stems is neatly staggered to minimize visual overlap.
evaluation.viewing_angle_rendered: The rendered screenshot shows a 3D stem plot from a similar elevation, but rotated so the 'n' axis runs downwards and to the right, and the 'm' axis runs upwards and to the right. This causes several stems to visually block each other.
evaluation.viewing_angle_comparison: The rendered view has a different azimuth/rotation than the source, causing the axes to align differently and forcing multiple labels (like 'm') to overlap directly with grid geometry.
discrepancies: The camera angle is rotated, making the layout of stems look disorganized and causing visual overlaps.
The 'm' label is placed inside the grid area instead of clearly outside at the end of its respective axis.
The tick labels (-1, 0, 1) are placed inside the grid cells, overlapping the gray lines and reducing readability.
The blue vertical stems are slightly too thick compared to the very fine lines in the original textbook figure.
evaluation.notes: The 3D stem plot accurately represents the mathematical concept, but suffers from incorrect camera rotation and severely cluttered labels overlapping grid geometry.
action_items: Adjust the camera azimuth to match the clean, staggered axonometric projection of the source figure.
Reposition the 'm' and 'n' axis labels and tick marks to the outer boundaries of the plot so they do not overlap grid lines.
Reduce the thickness of the vertical stem cylinders to mimic the fine hairline look of the original plot.
Add interactive controls (such as sliders to change the box filter size N, M or toggle normalization) to elevate the interactivity score.

feedback.rationale: The rendering suffers from poor camera rotation, cluttered/overlapping labels, thick stems, and a lack of interactivity, all of which are actionable implementation-level issues.
action_items: Adjust the camera azimuth to match the clean, staggered axonometric projection of the source figure.
Reposition the 'm' and 'n' axis labels and tick marks to the outer boundaries of the plot so they do not overlap grid lines.
Reduce the thickness of the vertical stem cylinders to mimic the fine hairline look of the original plot.
Add interactive controls (such as sliders to change the box filter size N, M or toggle normalization) to elevate the interactivity score.

attempts.html: ⚠ Figure failed to load

evaluation.viewing_angle_source: The source figure shows a 3D stem plot from a viewpoint that places the vertical value axis on the far-left. The horizontal 'm' axis runs from the front-left to back-right, and the 'n' axis runs from left to right along the bottom-right.
evaluation.viewing_angle_rendered: The rendered screenshot shows the plot from an angle where the vertical value axis is placed on the far-right, and the coordinate grid is rotated/mirrored relative to the original source.
evaluation.viewing_angle_comparison: The viewing angle in the rendered screenshot is rotated by approximately 180 degrees around the vertical axis, causing the vertical axis to appear on the right side instead of the left, and reversing the depth of the grid axes.

shape_comparison.source_shape: orthogonal lines forming a floor grid and partial back-planes
shape_comparison.rendered_shape: wireframe cage defining a 3D box support

discrepancies: The vertical axis is positioned on the right instead of the left due to an incorrect camera viewpoint.
The grid structure has extra wireframe cage lines enclosing the entire 3D volume, whereas the source has open, cleaner back planes.
Labels are extremely small and difficult to read.
Axis labels (n, m, and vertical axis) are positioned on the wrong sides of the plot.
evaluation.notes: The 3D scene is rotated incorrectly, placing the vertical axis on the wrong side, and the labels are too small to read.
action_items: Adjust the initial camera position (azimuth and elevation) so the vertical axis is on the left side of the plot as in the source figure.
Re-orient the n and m axes so their values (-1, 0, 1) and label directions match the original layout.
Increase the font size of all text labels and tick marks to improve legibility.
Simplify the grid geometry to remove the front and top bounding box wireframes, keeping only the floor grid and back-plane grid lines.
Add interactive UI controls, such as a dropdown to switch between different kernel types (e.g., Box, Gaussian, Identity) or a slider to adjust the kernel size.

feedback.rationale: The rendered 3D scene has an incorrect camera viewpoint, missing interactivity, cluttered grid geometry, and illegible labels, all of which are actionable items for the generator to fix.

Re-orient the n and m axes so their values (-1, 0, 1) and label directions match the original layout.

Simplify the grid geometry to remove the front and top bounding box wireframes, keeping only the floor grid and back-plane grid lines.
